section.heading: Foreign language proficiency: special pay for proficiency beneficial for other national security interests

subsection.heading: Authority.—
subsection.chapeau: The Secretary of Defense may pay special pay under this section to an employee of the Department of Defense who—

paragraph.content: has been certified by the Secretary to be proficient in a foreign language identified by the Secretary as being a language in which proficiency by civilian personnel of the Department is necessary because of national security interests;
is assigned duties requiring proficiency in that foreign language; and
is not receiving special pay under section 1596 of this title.

subsection.heading: Rate.—
subsection.content: The rate of special pay for an employee under this section shall be prescribed by the Secretary, but may not exceed five percent of the employee’s rate of basic pay.

subsection.heading: Relationship to Other Pay and Allowances.—
subsection.content: Special pay under this section is in addition to any other pay or allowances to which the employee is entitled.

subsection.heading: Regulations.—
subsection.content: The Secretary of Defense shall prescribe regulations to carry out this section.

note.heading: Amendments
note.p: 2004—Subsec. (a)(2). Pub. L. 108–375 struck out “during a contingency operation supported by the armed forces” after “foreign language”.

note.heading: Effective Date of 2004 Amendment
note.p: Pub. L. 108–375, div. A, title XI, § 1102(b), Oct. 28, 2004, 118 Stat. 2072, provided that: “The amendment by this section [amending this section] shall take effect on the first day of the first month that begins after the date of the enactment of this Act [Oct. 28, 2004].”
